text: Free passage seems to have been granted by the Edomites on this occasion (verse 29 below); the incident must not be confused with that described in Num. 20.14 sqq., when the Israelites were marching eastwards, not northwards.

It is not certain whether verses 20-23 are to be regarded as part of what God said to Moses, or as a footnote. ‘The Cappadocians’ is curiously used in the Latin to translate ‘the men of Caphtor’, that is, of Crete.
